description: Phase 2.5 corpus for citation-verifier refactor v0.3 status taxonomy. Each fixture declares an input citation and the expected verifier result under the six-status taxonomy in models.Status. Consumed by Phase 3 acceptance tests. See tests/data/refactor_corpus_survey.md for provenance and selection criteria.
selection_criteria: Each of the six statuses has at least 5 fixtures (hard minimum).
The named exemplars from design v2 §4 (Koch, Gilliam, Menges, WRONG_CASE, VERIFICATION_INCOMPLETE) are all present and tagged with category 'named_exemplar'.
Primary source: ~/Projects/case-law-proposition-benchmark/scratch/cl-coverage-offshoot/ — 250 ground-truthed citations with hand-classified diagnoses.
Secondary sources: tests/data/known_real_citations.json (anchors), tests/data/known_fake_citations.json (WRONG_CASE + hallucinated NOT_FOUND).
VERIFICATION_INCOMPLETE fixtures are synthetic (mock_spec field declares the simulated failure mode; Phase 3 builds the mock harness).
Provisional classifications use phase3_classification_open: true so Phase 3 can rule without re-curating.
expected_warnings_subset uses subset semantics — Phase 3 may add other warning categories without breaking the fixture.

fixtures.citation: Obergefell v. Hodges, 576 U.S. 644 (2015)
fixtures.rationale: Landmark SCOTUS case; primary citation_lookup resolves cleanly. Anchor fixture for the standard VERIFIED happy path.

fixtures.citation: Bossart v. King Cnty., Case No. 2:24-cv-01776-JHC, 2025 WL 459154, at *1 (W.D. Wash. Feb. 11, 2025)
fixtures.rationale: Pre-Phase-3 xfail (CL cluster-ID drift). Phase 3's caption_investigation may rescue. expected_final_ids.cluster_id pinned to the originally-canonical ID; Phase 3 confirms or updates. Abbreviation normalization (Cnty.) needed in the resolution path.

fixtures.citation: Gilliard v. McWilliams, 2019 WL 3304707
fixtures.rationale: Rule 25(d) automatic-substitution diverges the brief caption from CL's display caption (same case, different display name). citation_lookup resolves the WL cite cleanly; the name matcher must flag the mismatch as cl_display_name_data_bug rather than escalating to WRONG_CASE.

fixtures.citation: Preston v. Smith, 2023 WL 5337430
fixtures.rationale: Rule 25(d) substitution case. citation_lookup resolves; CL's display caption diverges from the brief caption. Second Rule 25(d) sample for sub-case variety.

fixtures.citation: Viken Detection Corp. v. Doe, 2019 WL 5268725
fixtures.rationale: Rule 25(d) substitution case. citation_lookup resolves; CL's display caption diverges from the brief caption. Third Rule 25(d) sample (all 3 from benchmark).

fixtures.citation: Michael B. v. Berryhill, 2019 WL 2269962
fixtures.rationale: Social Security Administration case using a plaintiff pseudonym (Michael B.) in the brief caption. CL stores the real plaintiff name (Buschman v. Berryhill). Same case, different display name. The name matcher must flag as cl_display_name_data_bug rather than WRONG_CASE.

fixtures.citation: John S. v. Bisignano, 2025 WL 1505405
fixtures.rationale: SSA case using a plaintiff pseudonym (John S.). CL stores the real plaintiff name (Sims v. Bisignano). Second ssa_pseudonym sample.

fixtures.citation: Koch v. United States, 857 F.3d 267 (5th Cir. 2017)
fixtures.rationale: Design v2 §4 named exemplar. Live citation_lookup against 857 F.3d 267 returns cluster 4390987 with CL case_name 'Ricky Koch v. Tote, Incorporated' — divergent from the brief's 'Koch v. United States' caption. The verifier's name matcher flags the mismatch; Phase 3's caption_investigation confirms it IS the same case (CL data-bug, not a wrong case).

fixtures.rationale: NY A.D.3d primary cite (230 A.D.3d 1113) is not in CL's citation index. The parallel NY Slip Op cite (2024 NY Slip Op 04376) resolves to cluster 10114438. Status: VERIFIED_PARTIAL — the case is verified, but coverage is incomplete because the primary cite didn't resolve.

fixtures.rationale: A.D.3d primary cite (235 A.D.3d 71) not in CL's index. Parallel slip op (2024 NY Slip Op 06432) resolves to cluster 10298541. Third parallel_cite_ny_adv sample.

fixtures.citation: People v. Walker, 228 A.D.3d 1318, 2024 NY Slip Op 03278 (N.Y. App. Div. 2024)
fixtures.rationale: A.D.3d primary cite (228 A.D.3d 1318) not in CL's index. Parallel slip op (2024 NY Slip Op 03278) resolves to cluster 9880367. Fifth parallel_cite_ny_adv sample (all 5 from benchmark).

fixtures.citation: Gilliam v. Uni Holdings, 201 A.D.3d 83, 88-89, 2021 NY Slip Op 06798 (N.Y. App. Div. 2021)
fixtures.rationale: Design v2 §4 named exemplar. A.D.3d primary cite (201 A.D.3d 83) not in CL's citation index. Parallel slip op (2021 NY Slip Op 06798) resolves to cluster 5305052 with CL case_name 'Gilliam v. Uni holdings'. Note: CL also has a duplicate cluster 5304049 for the same case_name + date_filed; Phase 3's deduper picks one canonical cluster. Status: VERIFIED_PARTIAL.

fixtures.citation: Mehar Holdings, LLC v. Evanston Ins. Co., 2016 WL 5957681 (W.D. Tex. Oct. 14, 2016)
fixtures.rationale: Design v2 §4 named VIA_RECAP exemplar (substituted, second-pass). Original Menges (2000 WL 765082) was substituted with Darensburg (2009 WL 2392094) in Phase 2.5; per Phase 3 §0.3 (Q1 Westlaw lookup), Darensburg's WL number actually maps to the Aug 4, 2009 procedural costs-taxation order (doc #460), NOT the July 7, 2009 attorneys' fees opinion (doc #452) the fixture originally pinned. Because Phase 3's strict VIA_RECAP gate (±14 days from cited date + opinion-typed keywords) cannot disambiguate, Darensburg is demoted to verified-docket-only-darensburg-wl-disambiguation. Mehar is the clean substitute: 2016-10-14 cited date matches RECAPDoc 18720567's entry_date_filed exactly, description 'OPINION on motion to dismiss' is opinion-typed under Phase 3 gating. Sub-case: recap_doc_opinion_not_ingested.

fixtures.citation: Doe v. Lawrence Gen. Hosp., 2025 WL 2808055
fixtures.rationale: WL cite. Opinion cluster not ingested; RECAP has the docket with a 'Memorandum & Order' RECAPDocument carrying plain_text. Status: VERIFIED_VIA_RECAP via recap_doc_opinion_not_ingested.

fixtures.citation: Darensburg v. Metro. Transp. Comm'n, 2009 WL 2392094 (N.D. Cal. Aug. 4, 2009)
fixtures.rationale: Documents a known Phase 3 limitation surfaced by §0.3 (Q1 Westlaw lookup). 2009 WL 2392094 actually maps to the Aug 4, 2009 procedural costs-taxation order (doc #460: 'ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR REVIEW OF CLERK'S TAXATION OF COSTS'), NOT the July 7, 2009 substantive attorneys' fees opinion (doc #452: 'OPINION ON DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES'). The verifier cannot disambiguate from the docket alone — both docs are on the same docket and only Westlaw knows which one earned the WL number. Under Phase 3 strict VIA_RECAP gating: doc #460 fails the opinion-typed test (procedural ORDER keywords) so it doesn't qualify as VIA_RECAP; doc #452 is outside the ±14 day window from cited Aug 4 (28-day delta) so it isn't considered. Result: DOCKET_ONLY. This fixture pins that behavior. Demoted from named-exemplar (was previously named-exemplar-menges); the named VIA_RECAP slot moved to Mehar Holdings.
fixtures.phase3_ruling: Reclassified VIA_RECAP -> DOCKET_ONLY per §0.3 Westlaw lookup. The cited WL number maps to a procedural order, not the substantive opinion the prior fixture assumed. Verifier has no way to know this from docket data alone.

fixtures.citation: Cabot v. Lewis, 2015 WL 13648107, at *1 (D. Mass. July 9, 2015)
fixtures.rationale: Provisional VIA_RECAP. Docket has an available RECAP document with plain_text, but the document is not strictly opinion-typed (description: 'ORDER CERTIFYING INTERLOCUTORY APPEAL'). Per plan §1.4 Phase 3 must decide whether 'has-text-but-not-opinion-typed' is VIA_RECAP or DOCKET_ONLY. Pinned to VIA_RECAP because the doc has substantial reasoned text; Phase 3 may reclassify to DOCKET_ONLY.

fixtures.citation: Hunter v. City & Cnty. of San Francisco, 2013 WL 6088409
fixtures.rationale: Provisional VIA_RECAP. Docket has a RECAP document with plain_text but not strictly opinion-typed (description starts 'ORDER RE: PLAINTIFFS MOTION FOR REVIEW OF CLERKS TAXATION OF...'). Per plan §1.4 Phase 3 gray area. Second recap_doc_not_opinion_typed sample.

fixtures.citation: ra dias deapocalypse bey v. Clapprood, 2024 WL 3446824
fixtures.rationale: Docket exists in RECAP but no available RECAP document for the cited entry (is_available=false on the best-match Memorandum & Order doc). No retrievable opinion text. Status: VERIFIED_DOCKET_ONLY via recap_doc_unavailable.

fixtures.citation: Hazari LLC v. Everest Indem. Ins. Co., 2020 WL 1969530 (S.D. Tex. Apr. 24, 2020)
fixtures.rationale: Docket exists in RECAP but the 'Order on Motion to Remand' best-match doc has is_available=false. No retrievable opinion text. Second recap_doc_unavailable sample.

fixtures.citation: Menges v. Cliffs Drilling Co., 2000 WL 765082 (E.D. La. May 31, 2000)
fixtures.rationale: Actual Menges v. Cliffs Drilling cite from design v2 §4. Live-discovered docket 10993603 (case_name 'Menges v. Cliffs Drlg Co'). Has 2 text-bearing 'ORDER & REASONS' docs from 2000-06-12 (motions in limine, doc_ids 476627754, 476627755) but NOT the cited 2000-05-31 opinion. Strict reading: no opinion-typed doc matches the cited date, so DOCKET_ONLY. Phase 3 may relax classification to VIA_RECAP if it accepts related in-limine orders as opinion-substitute text. phase3_classification_open: true.

fixtures.citation: Jacks v. Hertz Corp., 2020 WL 1530747 (Bankr. D. Del. Mar. 30, 2020)
fixtures.rationale: Live-discovered follow-up. WL cite (issued by D. Del. on 2020-03-30) resolves via recap_docket_search to CL docket 17228083 in the Bankr. N.D. Tex. (txnb) court — the case was transferred from D. Del. to txnb shortly after the WL opinion was issued, and CL's docket reflects the receiving court (docket_number 20-04044, filed 2020-06-05). No text-bearing docs found on first 20 entries (is_available=false across all checked docs). No retrievable opinion text. Status: VERIFIED_DOCKET_ONLY.

fixtures.citation: Caraballo v. Berryhill, 2018 WL 4677729
fixtures.rationale: Live-discovered follow-up. WL cite resolves via recap_docket_search to CL docket 6698093 (case_name 'Caraballo v. Berryhill', court S.D.N.Y. White Plains, docket_number 7:17-cv-07205-NSR-LMS, filed 2017-09-21 — the controller's initial candidate had mistakenly listed M.D. Tenn. as the court, corrected here by dropping the court parenthetical). The docket's only text-bearing doc is a 2021 attorney-fees OPINION (doc 183723709), not the cited 2018 SSA decision. Strict interpretation: no opinion text for cited date, so DOCKET_ONLY. Phase 3 may reclassify VIA_RECAP if it accepts a different opinion on the same docket. phase3_classification_open: true.

fixtures.citation: Hogan v. AT&T, Inc., 917 F. Supp. 1275, 1280 (S.D. Tex. 1994)
fixtures.rationale: Design v2 §4 named exemplar. Real reporter 917 F. Supp. 1275 resolves to cluster 2140439 (case_name 'U.S. ex rel. Green v. Washington', D.D.C. 1994), not 'Hogan v. AT&T'. Court-confirmed hallucination from Gonzalez v. TTRA sanctions order (S.D. Tex. 2025). Classic WRONG_CASE: real reporter, completely different parties. Per design §2.4, expected_final_ids point to the actual case the reporter resolves to, not the brief's fake name.

fixtures.citation: TIG Ins. Co. v. Carter, 640 S.W. 2d 232, 237 (Tex. 1982)
fixtures.rationale: Real reporter 640 S.W.2d 232 resolves to cluster 2418868 (case_name "Ogden v. Gibraltar Savings Ass'n", Tex. 1982 wrongful foreclosure case), not TIG Ins. Co. v. Carter. Court-confirmed wrong-name hallucination from Gonzalez v. TTRA sanctions order — same source as Hogan named exemplar. Second wrong_case_real_reporter sample.

fixtures.citation: Gallagher v. Wilton Enterprises, Inc., 962 F. Supp. 1162 (E.D. Pa. 1997)
fixtures.rationale: Real reporter 962 F. Supp. 1162 resolves to cluster 2311379 (case_name 'Kenro, Inc. v. Fax Daily, Inc.', S.D. Ind. 1997), not Gallagher v. Wilton Enterprises. Notable hybrid hallucination: 'Gallagher v. Wilton Enterprises' does exist as a real First Circuit opinion at a different citation, but the brief paired the real name with the wrong reporter number — court-confirmed hallucination from Nora v. M.A. Transport (N.D. Ill. 2025).

fixtures.citation: Shell Petroleum N.V. v. Republic of Costa Rica, 608 F. Supp. 2d 269 (S.D.N.Y. 2009)
fixtures.rationale: Real reporter 608 F. Supp. 2d 269 resolves to cluster 2467149 (case_name 'Faghri v. University of Connecticut', 2009), not Shell Petroleum v. Costa Rica. Court (Flowz Digital LLC, C.D. Cal. 2025) was unable to locate Shell Petroleum after multiple searches; the cited reporter belongs to an unrelated Faghri case. known_fake_citations.json originally tagged this as hallucinated_case, but it actually fits WRONG_CASE more cleanly because the reporter does resolve — to a wholly different case.

fixtures.citation: Butler Motors, Inc. v. Benosky, 181 N.E.3d 857 (Ind. Ct. App. 2021)
fixtures.rationale: Provisional WRONG_CASE per plan §4 footnote. The brief cites 181 N.E.3d 857 (wrong page); the actual case is at 181 N.E.3d 304 (same case, wrong page). However, controller live-verified that neither 181 N.E.3d 857 nor 181 N.E.3d 304 resolves to a CL cluster (both 404). Strict interpretation: status is NOT_FOUND, not WRONG_CASE, because the cite doesn't resolve to anything. Phase 3 may rule on whether 'wrong_page_number' is its own status, NOT_FOUND, or WRONG_CASE. expected_resolving_stage set to null since nothing resolves; expected_final_ids all null. phase3_classification_open: true.

fixtures.citation: Obergefell v. Hodges, 576 U.S. 644 (2015)
fixtures.rationale: Design v2 §4 named exemplar — the canonical reference fixture for the VERIFICATION_INCOMPLETE status. §4 specifies this case to test that a primary-stage infrastructure failure does NOT silently degrade to NOT_FOUND; the verifier must surface the error in resolution_path and emit VERIFICATION_INCOMPLETE. Simulated HTTP 500 from CourtListener on the primary citation_lookup endpoint. Per design §2.8 internal gate, the verifier must produce VERIFICATION_INCOMPLETE rather than silently degrade to NOT_FOUND. The input citation is real (Obergefell, cluster 2812209 would resolve cleanly under normal conditions) — the mock harness intercepts and substitutes the failure response. Anchor fixture for infrastructure-failure-on-primary-stage.

mock_spec.details: First (and only) attempted call to /api/rest/v4/citation-lookup/ returns HTTP 500 with empty body. Client.py does not retry 5xx errors (only 429); the stage records verdict=errored on the first attempt.

fixtures.citation: Bossart v. King Cnty., 2025 WL 459154 (W.D. Wash. Feb. 11, 2025)
fixtures.rationale: Simulated HTTP 429 from CourtListener on every retry attempt for citation_lookup, with no parseable Retry-After or wait_until field. The verifier's existing 429-retry logic in client.py exhausts after 3 attempts and the stage records verdict=errored. Status: VERIFICATION_INCOMPLETE. Exercises the rate-limit code path that already exists in client.py.

mock_spec.details: All 3 retries return HTTP 429 with empty body and no Retry-After header.

fixtures.citation: Anderson v. Furst, No. 17-cv-12676, 2018 WL 4407750 (E.D. Mich. Sept. 17, 2018)
fixtures.rationale: citation_lookup returns clean no_match (Anderson WL cite not in CL's citation index). opinion_search fallback times out at the 15s client timeout. Per design §2.8: a single late-stage timeout cannot silently degrade to NOT_FOUND. The resolution_path must record citation_lookup with verdict=no_match AND opinion_search with verdict=errored. Status: VERIFICATION_INCOMPLETE. Exercises the multi-stage path where primary succeeds (as no_match) but fallback fails.

mock_spec.details: citation_lookup returns a normal no_match response; the subsequent opinion_search fuzzy-fallback exceeds the 15s client timeout.

fixtures.citation: Hanover Shoe, Inc. v. United Shoe Machinery Corp., 392 U.S. 481 (1968)
fixtures.rationale: Simulated network-level connection error (e.g. DNS resolution failure, TCP RST) on the citation_lookup endpoint. The verifier's HTTP client raises ConnectionError; the stage records verdict=errored; status: VERIFICATION_INCOMPLETE. Input citation is a real SCOTUS case (Hanover Shoe, cluster 107749 under normal conditions) so the mock harness substitutes the failure cleanly.

mock_spec.details: First call to /api/rest/v4/citation-lookup/ raises a connection-level exception (DNS failure or TCP reset) before any response bytes.

fixtures.citation: Mehar Holdings, LLC v. Evanston Ins. Co., 2016 WL 5957681 (W.D. Tex. Oct. 14, 2016)
fixtures.rationale: Simulated malformed JSON response from recap_docket_search (citation_lookup + opinion_search both no_match; recap_docket_search returns HTTP 200 with body that fails json.loads). The verifier's parser raises JSONDecodeError; stage records verdict=errored. Status: VERIFICATION_INCOMPLETE. Input is Mehar Holdings (would normally resolve as VIA_RECAP, cluster null + docket 5474769 + doc 18720567); mock harness corrupts the recap_docket_search response.

mock_spec.details: citation_lookup and opinion_search return clean no_match responses. recap_docket_search returns HTTP 200 but the response body is truncated JSON (e.g. missing closing brace) that fails json.loads.
